data.title: WBTC Transfer from Solv Protocol to Winslow Strong
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, data from Arkham indicates that at 22:09, a total of 32.11 WBTC, valued at approximately $2.91 million, was transferred from Solv Protocol to Winslow Strong.

data.title: Google Chrome Extension Secretly Charges Fees on Solana Transactions
data.description:
According to Odaily, a Google Chrome extension has been discovered to secretly charge users fees during transactions on the Solana blockchain. A report released by cybersecurity firm Socket on Tuesday revealed that the extension, known as Crypto Copilot, injects additional transfers into each Solana Swap, stealing at least 0.0013 SOL or 0.05% of the transaction amount. 

Crypto Copilot utilizes the decentralized exchange Raydium to execute swaps for users but adds a secondary instruction to transfer SOL from the user to the attacker's wallet. The user interface only displays the swap details, while the wallet confirmation screen summarizes the transaction information without showing specific instructions.

Crypto Copilot is marketed as a convenient tool for Solana traders to execute swaps directly via Twitter.

data.title: Top 100 Companies Hold Over 1 Million BTC as of November 2025
data.description: According to Odaily, as of November 27, 2025, the top 100 publicly listed companies with Bitcoin treasuries collectively hold 1,058,581 BTC. Over the past week, nine companies increased their Bitcoin holdings, while one company reduced its holdings.

data.title: Do Kwon Asks for Five Years Prison After $40B Terra Collapse – What About SBF?
data.description:
Do Kwon requests five years in prison for orchestrating Terra's $40 billion collapse, arguing lengthy Montenegro detention warrants leniency despite prosecutors agreeing to recommend up to 12 years under his guilty plea.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/do-kwon-asks-for-five-years-prison-after-40b-terra-collapse-what-about-sbf/

data.title: Security firm: Malicious Google Chrome browser extensions can secretly steal some SOL tokens.
data.description: A Google Chrome extension allows users to trade on Solana while secretly extracting a portion of the transaction fees into the creator's wallet. According to a report released Tuesday by cybersecurity firm Socket, Crypto Copilot injects extra funds into each Solana Swap, stealing at least 0.0013 SOL, or 0.05% of the transaction amount. On the backend, Crypto Copilot uses the decentralized exchange Raydium to execute the swap for the user, but appends a second instruction to transfer SOL from the user to the attacker. The user interface only displays the swap details, while the wallet confirmation screen "only summarizes the transaction information and does not display specific instructions." Crypto Copilot is reportedly a convenient tool that allows Solana traders to execute swap transactions directly via Twitter. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: BlackRock Bitcoin ETF Investors Return to Profit as Bitcoin Surges
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, Cointelegraph reports that holders of BlackRock's spot Bitcoin ETF, IBIT, have returned to profitability following Bitcoin's rise above $90,000. This development suggests a potential shift in sentiment among key investor groups driving the market this year.

Data from Arkham indicates that holders of BlackRock's IBIT have seen cumulative profits rebound to $3.2 billion. Arkham noted that on October 7, holders of IBIT and ETHA collectively achieved nearly $40 billion in profits at their peak, which had dropped to $630 million four days earlier. This implies that the average cost of all IBIT purchases is nearly balanced. As ETF holders face less pressure, the pace of Bitcoin ETF sell-offs may continue to slow. Since the recorded net outflow of $903 million on November 20, the situation has significantly improved.

data.title: Binance to Introduce APRO (AT) Across Multiple Services
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the platform is set to integrate APRO (AT) into several of its services, including Binance Simple Earn, "Buy Crypto," Binance Convert, and Binance Margin. The Earn AT Flexible Products will be available for subscription on Binance Simple Earn starting 2025-11-27 at 14:00 (UTC). This initiative is part of the Binance HODLer Airdrops program, which rewards BNB holders with token airdrops based on historical snapshots of their BNB Simple Earn asset or BNB On-Chain Yield balances. Users can subscribe to BNB Simple Earn Products or BNB On-Chain Yields to automatically benefit from HODLer Airdrops rewards.

In the "Buy Crypto" section, users will have the option to purchase AT using various payment methods, including VISA, MasterCard, Google Pay, Apple Pay, and Revolut. Additionally, they can buy and sell AT using their account balances on the "Buy Crypto" page, which will be accessible within one hour of AT's listing on Binance Spot. For Binance Convert, users will be able to trade AT against BTC, USDT, and other tokens at zero fees, also within one hour of its listing on Binance Spot.

On the Binance Margin platform, AT will be introduced as a new borrowable asset on both Cross and Isolated Margin, with the AT/USDT and AT/USDC pairs available for trading. This will be effective from 2025-11-27 at 14:00 (UTC). The same applies to Portfolio Margin, where AT will be added as a borrowable asset along with the AT/USDT and AT/USDC pairs. Binance advises users to exercise caution and implement stringent risk management strategies when trading newly listed tokens due to potential volatility.

data.title: Infinex will launch a public offering on Sonar, aiming to raise $15 million.
data.description: Infinex will launch a public sale on Sonar, allowing community members to acquire INX tokens before the project's January 2026 token sale, TGE. Last year, Infinex raised $67.7 million through a Patron NFT sale. In a podcast, Infinex founder Kain Warwick stated that by launching the public sale on Sonar, Infinex plans to sell 5% of the total INX supply at a fixed, fully diluted valuation of $300 million, aiming to raise $15 million.

data.title: Visa Expands Stablecoin Settlement in CEMEA Region
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, Visa is broadening its use of stablecoins for transaction settlements in Central and Eastern Europe, the Middle East, and Africa (CEMEA) through a collaboration with crypto infrastructure firm Aquanow. Announced on Thursday, the partnership aims to utilize approved stablecoins like USDC to streamline transactions by reducing costs, operational friction, and settlement times. This initiative responds to the growing demand from banks and payment firms for more efficient cross-border payment solutions. By leveraging stablecoins, Visa intends to digitize the backend of money movement, enabling 24/7 settlement capabilities. Godfrey Sullivan, Visa’s head of product and solutions for the CEMEA region, emphasized that this integration will allow institutions in the area to benefit from faster and simpler settlements. He stated that the partnership with Aquanow is a significant step in modernizing payment systems, reducing dependency on traditional methods with multiple intermediaries, and preparing institutions for the future of financial transactions.

Stablecoins, initially used by cryptocurrency users for transferring funds between exchanges, have evolved to play a role similar to the US dollar in the on-chain economy. Their use is expanding beyond the crypto-native world, now encompassing institutional settlements and payments. In a related development, Deutsche Börse, a market infrastructure provider, announced plans to integrate the EURAU euro-pegged stablecoin issued by AllUnity. This move is part of the exchange group's broader digital-asset strategy, which includes previous collaborations with Circle’s Euro Coin (EURC) and Societe Generale-Forge’s EUR CoinVertible (EURCV). Deutsche Börse plans to incorporate EURAU into its institutional custody service and aims for future integration of the euro stablecoin across its entire service portfolio.

Regulatory discussions continue regarding the classification and supervision of stablecoin exposure within the banking system. Erik Thedéen, governor of the Swedish central bank and chair of the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision, acknowledged the potential need for a revised approach to the current 1,250% risk weighting for crypto exposures. Meanwhile, Bank of England Deputy Governor Sarah Breeden indicated that the UK intends to align with the United States on stablecoin regulation, suggesting that major jurisdictions may proceed in tandem as stablecoins become more integrated into payment and settlement systems.

data.title: Visa doubles down on stablecoins in Europe, Middle East, Africa with new partnership
